task1.title: Letter to a Library
task1.prompt:
You recently borrowed a book from your local library and have lost it. Write a letter to the library. In your letter:
• explain what happened to the book
• apologise for losing it
• say what you will do about it
task1.sampleBand7:
Dear Library Manager,

I am writing regarding a book I borrowed last month — 'The Great Gatsby' by F. Scott Fitzgerald (Library ID: LB-4421). I regret to inform you that I have been unable to locate it despite conducting a thorough search of my home.

I sincerely apologise for the inconvenience this has caused. I believe the book may have been accidentally misplaced during a recent house move, which makes retrieval unlikely.

To resolve this matter promptly, I would like to replace the missing copy at my own expense. Please let me know the cost of a replacement, and I will arrange payment at the earliest opportunity. I would also be happy to visit in person if that would be more convenient.

Once again, I apologise unreservedly for this unfortunate situation.

Yours faithfully,
Alex Chen

task1.title: Complaint About a Noisy Neighbour
task1.prompt:
You have been experiencing problems with noise from your neighbour. Write a letter to your landlord. In your letter:
• describe the problem
• explain how it is affecting you
• suggest what you would like the landlord to do

task2.title: Social Media and Society
task2.prompt: Some people believe that social media has had a largely negative effect on society. Others argue that it has brought many benefits. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.

task2.title: Working from Home
task2.prompt: Many companies now allow employees to work from home. Do you think the advantages of this outweigh the disadvantages?
